text: R&D investment remains a strategic priority for Amazon, with FY2025 R&D expenses reaching $108,521,000,000 [CHUNK_ID: 534dacac-29c9-481c-8845-b786f27f197d], up from $88,544,000,000 in FY2024 [CHUNK_ID: a34693a5-e557-42e6-92f5-aa5998f8da7f], $85,622,000,000 in FY2023 [CHUNK_ID: 2fdd530a-a7d5-4c8e-aaa5-10ab743173e5], and $73,213,000,000 in FY2022 [CHUNK_ID: 0eece3ef-3e1b-4c42-935a-1d34e429cf70]. FY2022 R&D intensity was 14.24% of revenue [CALC_ID: 067e0511-a49a-4cdb-847f-c0470722fc7b], while FY2025 R&D intensity appears to have increased to approximately 15.1% based on the revenue and R&D figures provided. This elevated R&D spending intensity indicates continued investment in AWS infrastructure, AI capabilities, and logistics automation to sustain competitive advantages across Amazon's diversified business portfolio.

issues.explanation: The as-of date is 2025-07 (July 2025), but the paragraph presents FY2025 as a completed fiscal year with full-year data. Amazon's fiscal year ends in December, so FY2025 full-year financial data would not be available until early 2026. This presents future/completed year data as historical fact when only partial year data should exist at the as-of date.
issues.recommended_fix: Either clarify that FY2025 figures are projections/estimates, or adjust to reference only available periods (e.g., TTM or H1 2025). Verify the fiscal year end date and ensure data availability aligns with the as-of date.

issues.offending_span: FY2025 R&D intensity appears to have increased to approximately 15.1% based on the revenue and R&D figures provided
issues.explanation: The paragraph claims FY2025 R&D intensity of 15.1% but provides no cited calculation result or revenue chunk to support this figure. Unlike FY2022 intensity (which has CALC_ID citation), there is no evidence source for the FY2025 intensity calculation. The FY2025 revenue figure needed for this calculation is not cited.
issues.recommended_fix: Add a calculation result ID showing the FY2025 R&D intensity computation with the corresponding FY2025 revenue figure, or remove the specific percentage if it cannot be verified.

issues.offending_span: This elevated R&D spending intensity indicates continued investment in AWS infrastructure, AI capabilities, and logistics automation
issues.explanation: This analytical claim about what the R&D spending is specifically invested in (AWS infrastructure, AI capabilities, logistics automation) is not supported by any cited evidence chunks. The chunks only show R&D expense amounts, not the breakdown or purpose of the spending.
issues.recommended_fix: Either cite specific evidence chunks that detail R&D spending allocation, or qualify this as analyst interpretation rather than stated fact (e.g., 'suggests potential investment in...' or 'consistent with Amazon's stated priorities in...').
